mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, equity discount rates decline and risk appetite improves, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. This signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating sharply, because falling policy rates then reflect easing rather than crisis.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with declining FEDFUNDS do not produce higher 20d forward SPX returns than flat or rising FEDFUNDS periods, or if the signal turns negative when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold marks labor-market deterioration that lowers expected earnings and increases recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should appear after the unemployment rate has clearly moved higher, not when it is merely noisy month to month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes of UNRATE acceleration are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both weaken together, household demand is losing momentum across consumption and housing, which should depress cyclical earnings expectations and weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The joint deterioration matters more than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform either single-series decline as a predictor of negative 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not statistically stronger than each proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial supply and production momentum, which supports forward earnings and cyclical sentiment. In normal expansion conditions, stronger industrial production should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly or short-run changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5d SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal disappears after controlling for contemporaneous market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above the recent tightening threshold compresses valuation multiples and raises recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest once policy has moved into restrictive territory rather than during the initial hiking phase.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not have lower 20d SPX returns than periods below that threshold, or if high-rate episodes still produce positive returns after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with low UNRATE identifies an intact labor market that supports household income and equity earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect depends on both employment growth and unemployment remaining subdued.
failure_condition: Reject if high PAYEMS growth fails to improve 60d SPX returns when UNRATE is low, or if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction adds no predictive power over either variable alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL inflation trend erodes real purchasing power and forces tighter financial conditions, which should pressure equity multiples and lower S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should be clearest when inflation is accelerating rather than merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal weakens after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-tightening rebound, improving GDPC1 alongside rising HOUST signals that real activity and interest-sensitive demand are recovering together, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when GDP rebounds but housing remains weak, because then the recovery is too narrow to support broad equities.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and HOUST does not lead to higher 20d SPX returns than GDP-only improvements, or if the combined signal is not positive in post-tightening recoveries.
